reasoning: accesslock_ID is declared as bigint(20) NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT in setup.php (line 470) — a MySQL-generated integer with no external input path. The data flow is: MySQL AUTO_INCREMENT -> $wpdb->get_results() -> $aRow->accesslock_ID -> HTML attribute. No attacker-controlled value ever populates this column; MySQL enforces the integer type at the storage layer and rejects non-integer inserts. The sink is also behind check_ajax_referer('wpcaptcha_run_tool') and current_user_can('manage_options'), so only authenticated administrators can reach it. The 'corruption scenario' the finding relies on (a non-integer ending up in an AUTO_INCREMENT bigint) is outside a realistic threat model. Absence of esc_attr() is a coding style gap, not an exploitable vulnerability — the value is guaranteed to be a positive integer string and cannot break HTML attribute context.
